founder: Archbishop Alfonso de Fuesca de Seville

description:
Located near the site of Kalmar's medieval harbor, it has played a crucial part in Swedish history since its initial construction as a fortified tower in the 12th century. King Magnus Ladulås ordered the construction of a fortress around the tower in the 1280s, and was further expanded in the centuries that followed.
One of the most significant political events in Scandinavia took place at Kalmar Castle in 1397, when the Kalmar Union was formed - a union of Denmark, Norway and Sweden, organized by Queen Margaret I of Denmark. During the Swedish rebellion against Denmark in 1520, the fortress was commanded by Anna Eriksdotter.
The fortress was improved during the 16th century under the direction of King Gustav I and his sons Eric XIV and John III, who turned the medieval fortress into a castle fit for a renaissance king. Today, it is one of Sweden's best preserved renaissance castles and is open to the public.

remarkable: Floris V werd op een jachtpartij bij het Muiderslot vermoord. Dichter P.C. Hooft was drost van Muiden in de 17de-eeuw.
description: Tolburcht bij de monding van de Vecht in de voormalige Zuiderzee. Na de dood van Floris V in 1296 werd het Muiderslot verwoest en in de 14de eeuw weer opgebouwd.
Het kasteel is aanzienlijk uitgebreid in de 14de eeuw door Archbishop Burkhard von Weisspriach.
In het hoogste deel van de burcht bevinden zich restanten van een 12de-eeuwse toren en bevindt zich een 15de-eeuws gotisch paleis.
De constructie bestaat uit een oud en een nieuw pauselijk paleis verdedigd door hoge weermuren met vierkante hoektorens.

founder: King Ludovic I D'Anjou of Hongarije
remarkable: Vlad Tepes, die Bram Stoker inspireerde voor zijn Graaf Dracula, zou hier in de 15de eeuw gevangen zijn gehouden. Het echte kasteel van Vlad de spietser is een ruine in Vallachia: Poenari.

De Zwarte Toren bevat een "oubliette", een geheime kerker met alleen een opening in het plafond op vier meter hoogte. De noodlottigen die moesten verdwijnen werden in het gat gegooid en letterlijk vergeten en aan hun lot overgelaten. Nog twee andere "oubliettes" die zijn gevonden bevonden zich in Pierrefonds en de Bastille. De toren is zo genoemd omdat het daglicht alleen via de schietgaten naar binnen kon komen. Later is de toren als gevangenis gebruikt en werden zelfs de schietgaten gedicht waardoor het letterlijk alles zwart werd in de toren.
description: Gelegen aan het smalste punt van de Bosporus aan de europese kant, tegenover Anadoli Hissar. Gebouwd door de grieken tegen invallen vanuit de Zwarte Zee.

In de 17de-eeuw is de noordvleugel gesloopt vrij zicht vanaf het terras te krijgen.

description: Visborg (Wisborg) refers to a fortress in the town of Visby on the Swedish island of Gotland. There was no one fortress called "Visborg", rather it refers to successive fortresses built in Visby (Borg in Old Norse means fortress or castle). Though Visborg is usually a reference to the castle built here by Erik of Pomerania.

remarkable: Geboorteplaats van koning Henry VII (1485-1509).

description: Also known as Hunyadi's Castle, Huniazi Castle and Corvin's Castle

The castle was purposefully build on an small rocky island in the middle of a deep and very rapidly flowing straight between 2 lake systems, which was frequently used as a waterway for trading and war during history. This also meant that the castle's "moat" (the straight itself) would not freeze over during even the harshest of winters. There was a well for sweetwater on the island, though it had to be dug unseemly deep and was even then prone to drying up at times.

The castle had originally 5 nearly equally tall round towers built to similar design like those on your picture (cannon towers), of which 1 was partially dismantled due to perceived danger of imminent collapse and the other one in fact did collapse due to neglect, they were located on the castle's "backside" where the water was wider (upper lake) and so were not rebuilt but were replaced with bastion defences later on.

The original castle was triangular (not square) in shape and had 3 towers in each of it corners 2 remain and 1 was destroyed.

Later on the castle was expanded first by adding 2 more towers and curtain walls to cover the islands only water source (the well) now covered by the huge white canvas. This also allowed more substantial storage space and accommodations to be placed secure on the island. One of the additional round towers is still seen on the upper-right corner of the castle here, the other (named the "fat tower" due its originally 5 meter thick walls, since it was intended to be used, as a second "keep" at the time) is now covered by the huge bastion seen in the lower-right corner of the castle, the defences facing the upper lake were later on strengthened with further 2 large bastions and 2 additional small ones added in the upper left-corner and the middle of the right flank of the castle closely adjoining the original "Fat Tower" or the "Grand Bastion" as it became known. The original triangular Medieval Castle served as the "Keep" for the whole complex, with its walls maintained as clearly the highest in all the castle. 

"The Castle Front" side facing the town of Savonlinna (meaning "the castle of Savolax", the town was named after the province this castle and its town administered and protected). 
[Text: Ilari Rahja]

castle_name: Saone (Citadel of Salah Ed-Din, Saladin's Burcht)
remarkable: One of the most magnificent features of the fortress is the 28 m deep ditch, which was cut into living rock probably by the Byzantines (it might have been completed by the Crusaders). This ditch, which runs 156 meters along the east side, is 14 to 20 meters wide and has a lonely 28 m high needle to support the drawbridge.
description: Emperor John I Tzimiskes gained control of the place from the Aleppan Hamdanid dynasty, and built the first of its defensive structures. It then fell in the hands of the Crusaders at around the beginning of the 12th century. It is mentioned that in 1119 it was owned by Robert of Saone who was given control of it by Roger, Prince of Antioch. Most of what is evident today was built at this time. The fortress was notable as being one of the few which were not entrusted to the major military orders of the Hospitaller and the Templars. The Crusader walls were breached by the armies of Muslim leader Salah ed-Din in July 1188, and it is from this victory that the castle takes its present name.
De mottebucht binnen het kasteel is gebouwd door King William II of England in de 1097.

area: South Europe, Alps, Holy Roman Empire

description: Until the 14th century, the site was known as the grad of Moscow. The word "kremlin" was first recorded in 1331 and its etymology is disputed (see Max Vasmer online (Russian)). The grad was greatly extended by Prince Yuri Dolgorukiy in 1156, destroyed by the Mongols in 1237 and rebuilt in oak in 1339.

A fortress was built at the location around 1380 by Bo Jonsson Grip, and belonged to his family until the confiscation of mansions and castles by King Gustav I in 1526. The King tore it down, and built a fortified castle with circular corner towers and a wall, for defensive purposes. Of the original medieval fortress, only the façade of a wall remains.

The castle is situated on the limestone cliff, and has been constructed in place of a Slavs settlement and from the 11th century it has become the king's guard castle of the town. The oldest part of the castle is probably the Romanesque tower. The rests of the tower are found in the advanced courtyard. The castle was owned by important royal and gentry families, of which the most famous was Matúš Cák of Trencín (from the beginning of the 14th century).

Glimmingehus, located in Simrishamn Municipality, Skåne County in southern Sweden, is the best preserved medieval castle in Scandinavia. It was built 1499-1506, during an era when Scania formed a vital part of Denmark, and contains many defensive arrangements of the era, such as parapets, false doors and dead-end corridors, 'murder-holes' for pouring boiling pitch over the attackers, moats, drawbridges and various other forms of death traps to surprise trespassers and protect the nobles against peasant uprisings. The lower part of the castle's stone walls are 2.4 meters (94 inches) thick and the upper part 1.8 meters (71 inches).

description: Clifford's Tower is the remaining keep of York Castle. The castle was originally built of wood around 1068 by William I (William the Conqueror). It was burnt to the ground in 1190.  The present Tower was completed in 1259, as part of Henry III's comprehensive rebuild of the castle in stone. The Tower later became known as Clifford's Tower after Roger de Clifford, who was hanged there in 1322.

remarkable:
Countess Elizabeth Báthory de Ecsed (17 August 1560 – 21 August 1614) was a countess from the renowned Báthory family. Although in modern times she has been labeled the most prolific female serial killer in history, evidence of her alleged crimes is scant and her guilt is debated. Nevertheless, she is remembered as the "Blood Countess" and as the "Bloody Lady of Čachtice", after the castle near Trencsén (today Trenčín) in the Kingdom of Hungary (today's Slovakia), where she spent most of her adult life.
After her husband's death, she and four collaborators were accused of torturing and killing hundreds of girls and young women, with one witness attributing to them over 600 victims, though the number for which they were convicted was 80. Elizabeth herself was neither tried nor convicted. In 1610, however, she was imprisoned in the Csejte Castle, where she remained bricked in a set of rooms until her death four years later. Reviled as the World's Worst Female Serial Killer, Countess Báthory is said to have bathed in the blood of the 650 servant girls she tortured and murdered. Reputed to be a vampire, lesbian, and witch, her shocking story inspired the Brothers Grimm, Bram Stoker, and gothic horror fans around the world for the past four hundred years.

description: Only the "Burgtor" tower and barbican remains of the town castle.

founder: King Haakon V Magnuson of Norway
description:
Bohus Fortress (also Baahus or Båhus, originally: Bagahus) lies along the old Norwegian - Swedish border in Kungälv, Bohuslän, Sweden, north east from Hisingen where the Göta river splits into two branches (20 kilometres north of Gothenburg). It commands its surroundings from a 40 meter high cliff, with the river forming a natural moat about it.
According to Kavli, “by 1310 records show it was constructed, as normal for that period, out of granite and brick, perhaps under the guidance of Count Jacob of Halland. By 1450 it included a continuous surrounding wall, 3 meter thick at the base, with a height which varied from 8.5 to 13.5 meters, varying with the terrain. It was approximately rectangular, with four rectangular corner towers. At the eastern end there was a brick tower, and in the center of the west side a gate house and drawbridge. Along the inside of the surrounding wall buildings were located which among other things included the "Kings hall," the castle commander’s residence, the chapel, the guardroom, the barracks and the kitchen. The fortress had secure vaulted positions, partly cut into the mountain, and beyond that strong outer-works. At the time Båhus was Norway's strongest fortress. The approaches were very difficult and the area to be defended was small, only 250 x 150 meters, so it did not require a large defensive force.

remarkable: Kula Nehaj, Nehajgrad

Český Krumlov Castle is unusually large for a town of Krumlov's size; within the Czech Republic it is second in extent only to the Hradčany castle complex of Prague. Inside its grounds are a large garden, an extensive bridge over a deep gap in the rock upon which the castle is built and the castle itself, which in turn consist of many defined parts dating from different periods of time.

Žužemberk's history goes back to the 13th century. Its defenses were reinforced with seven huge round towers in the 16th century. From 1538 until World War II, it was owned by the noble Auersperg family from Turjak and Šumberk. During the war, the castle was heavily bombarded, but restorations began in the 1960’s and the castle now hosts cultural and tourist events in the summer.

castle_name: Al-Qala'a al-Gabal (Citadel of the Mountain, Al Muqattam)
founder: Al-Malik al-Nasir Salah al-Din al-Ayyubi (also known as Saladin, r. AD 1169–93).
remarkable: THe largest tower in the CItadel is Burg al-Muqattam, built in 16th century. It is 24 m in diameter and designed to withstand artillery attacks.
description: The Citadel of Salah al-Din al-Ayyubi, known also as Citadel of the Mountain (Qala'a al-Jabal), is one of the most important landmarks of Cairo and amongst the most imposing military citadels to be built in the Middle Ages. Having assumed power in Egypt Salah al-Din al-Ayyubi (Saladin) noted that despite its grandeur and vastness Cairo did not have a citadel to protect her like other great cities in the Levant such as Damascus and Aleppo. He therefore began to construct the citadel, ordering construction of its walls, semi-circular towers and its two gates: Bab al-Qarafa and Bab al-Mudarraj. Saladin's son, Al-Malik al-‘Adil (r. AH 596–615 / AD 1200–18) completed the building of the citadel and surrounded some of the towers with outer encasing towers, in order to increase their impregnability, these included three towers: Burg al-Imam, Burg al-Ramla and Burg al-Haddad. He also added two square towers: Burg al-Turfa and Burg al-Karkialan. These works were completed in AH 604 / AD 1207.

castle_name: Hrad Loket Burg Elbogen an der Eger, Elnbogen)

description:
The Norman keep was built on a high motte on the site of a Roman castra, first uncovered during the third Marquess of Bute's building campaign. The Norman keep, of which the shell remains, was constructed about 1091 by Robert Fitzhamon, lord of Gloucester and conqueror of Glamorgan. 
In the early 19th century the castle was enlarged and refashioned in an early Gothic Revival style for John Crichton-Stuart, 2nd Marquess of Bute by Henry Holland. But its transformation began in 1868 when 3rd Marquess of Bute commissioned William Burges to undertake a massive rebuilding which turned the castle into a 19th century fantasy of a medieval palace, with a series of rooms that, perhaps, constitute the highest achievement of later Victorian Gothic Revival design.

remarkable: Ridder Hans "Trapp" von Drodt was in de 15de eeuw een gevreesde bewoner van het kasteel. Vogelvrij verklaard door de paus en de keizer en in strijd met de omliggende dorpen vormt hij nog steeds een hoofdpersoon in vele legenden.
description: De burcht is volledig gebouwd in en op een grote rotspartij. De oorspronkelijke ingang werd gevormd door een natuurlijke schacht in de rots waardoor het bijna onmogelijk was de burcht te veroveren. En brand in 1591 verwoeste de gebouwen van het kasteel waardoor het in 1689 als enige (?) kasteel in de Elzas niet is opgeblazen in opdracht van koning Lodewijk XIV van Frankrijk. In 1922 kwam het kasteel in privebezit en werd het opnieuw opgebouwd.
Van het oorspronkelijke 14de eeuwse kasteel staat alleen nog de ronde toren overeind. De rest is in de 17de-eeuw opnieuw opgebouwd en uitgebreid met de bijgebouwen.

remarkable: Dunvegan bezit een natte variant van een oubliette: In de "bottle dungeon" werden gevangenen gegooid om te verdrinken.
description: Oorspronkelijk was het kasteel allen per boot te bereiken.
Originally royal castle, then owned by nobles, destroyed during Swedish invasion in 1655 (II North War)

description: Castle of knights familly Teczynski, rebuilt in 16th century in renaissance style, since 17th century owned by nobles familly: Lubomirscy, Sieniawscy, Czartoryscy. Destroyed by fire in 1768 then abandoned.
Teutonic castle from 13th century, then owned by Prussian prince, ruined during II north war, by fire in 18th century and Napoleon’s wars, bombed by Russians during World war II, rebuilt in 60’s

area: Eastern Europe, Holy Roman Empire, Crusades
founder: Bishop of Varmia: Henryk II Wogenap
description: Castle and cathedral founded by Henryk Wogenap, owned by bishops of Varmia, Nicolas Copernicus has writen here “de revolutionibus orbium coelestium”, destroyed by Russians during WWII then rebuilt.

remarkable: This castle was represented in the famous book "Très riches heures" of  Duc de Berry (15th century) in the month August.
description: In de 15de eeuw is het kasteel vernietigd in opdracht van koning Henry VII. Van het oorspronkelijke kasteel resteert slechts de donjon, "la tour royale".

remarkable: The Citadel is situated at the entrance of the eastern harbour on the eastern point of the Pharos Island. It was erected on the exact site of the famous Lighthouse of Alexandria, which was one of the Seven Wonders of the Ancient World.
description: The Qaitbay Citadel in Alexandria is considered one of the most important defensive strongholds, not only in Egypt, but also along the Mediterranean Sea coast. It formed an important part of the fortification system of Alexandria in the 15th century A.D.

area: South Europe, Alps, Holy Roman Empire

remarkable: In de 17e eeuw kreeg Loevestein de status van staatsgevangenis. De bekendste gevangene was Hugo de Groot. Hij ontsnapte op 22 maart 1622 in een boekenkist en vluchtte naar Parijs.

description: Castle built by Silesian knights, rebuilt in renaissance in 16th century, ruined by Russians in 1726 and fire in 1876, duringWWII it was used as a German war factory

Castle built by teutonic knights, then owned by bishops of Varmia, rebuilt in renaissance style in 16th century, transformed into prison by prussians in 18th century, then destroyed by fire in 1807, restored in 1829 by evangelic county and in 1980 museum and hotel by foundation “Pojezierze”.

founder: Robrecht de Fries, Count of Flanders

description: Verwoest in de 16de eeuw door spaanse troepen.

place: northeastern end of Loch Awe, Oban 29 km (Argyll & Bute)

remarkable: Located next to Chateau de Ratsamhausen.

castle_name: Quala'at Masyaf (Assassin's Castle, Hashshashin or Nizari)

remarkable: In gebruik als gerechtshof.
description: Door Spanjaarden in 16de eeuw verwoest (brand). In 17de eeuw herbouwd.
Het kasteel was oorspronkelijk dubbel omgracht. Voor het kasteel staan twee eenzame vierkante torentjes, die deel hebben uitgemaakt van de vroegere voorgebouwen van het kasteel.

castle_name: Citadel of Ani (Palace of the Bagratids)
remarkable: A thousand years ago Ani was the  capital of an Armenian kingdom that covered much of present day Armenia and eastern Turkey.
description: At the southern end of Ani the ground rises up to form a flat-topped hill. This part of Ani is known as the "Inner Fortress" (Midjnaberd in Armenian), or simply as the "citadel". As well as having natural defences of steep cliffs on three sides, the citadel was encircled by a single line of ramparts. This wall had towers on its north facing section, and an entrance gateway at its north-western corner.

remarkable: Jacoba van Beieren, gravin van Holland, Zeeland en Henegouwen, is hier in 1436 overleden.

castle_name: Château de la Bastille (Prison de la Bastille)
remarkable: Het kasteel bevatte een "oubliette", een geheime kerker met alleen een opening in het plafond. De noodlottigen die moesten verdwijnen werden in het gat gegooid en letterlijk vergeten en aan hun lot overgelaten.
description: Het kasteel is verwoest tijdens de opstand van de parijzenaren in de franse revolutie (1789).
Castle build by Bishop Drzewiecki, then owned by his familly and other nobles, in XVIII used by female Bernandines Order, destroyed by fire In 1814
De donjon is uit de 15de-eeuw. Het kasteel is aanzienlijk verbouwd in de 18de-eeuw.

castle_name: Castillo de San Juan de la Suda de Tortosa

description: Het oorspronkelijke kasteel is in de tweede wereldoorlog bijna compleet verwoest. De huidige donjon is een betonnen reconstructie.

remarkable: Dit kasteel speelde een bijrol in de serie van Floris als de tolburcht van Floris.
description: Het kasteel bezit intacte -voor Nederland unieke- overdekte weergangen. Van de donjon is alleen nog een restant over.

founder: Rodrigo Diaz de Vivar y Mendoza

castle_name: Château de la Roche En Ardenne

remarkable: Strategisch gelegen bij een eiland in de Seine tussen Normandië en het franse koninkrijk.
description: Het kasteel bestaat uit drie delen: Een hoofdburcht aan de voet van de berg, die via een onderaardse gang verbonden is met een ommuurde donjon boven op de berg en een motteburcht die het plateau bovenop de berg controleerde.

area: South Europe, Alps, Holy Roman Empire

description: In de 15de eeuw zijn de ronde torens toegevoerd door bisschop David van Bourgondië.
De toren is in de 13de eeuw bijgebouwd door koning Richard I

founder: Emperor Charles IV of the holy roman empire

description: A mound in the grounds of the castle is all that remains of an earlier 12th century motte. The 14th century keep is ruined. What remains is the 15th century castle.

remarkable: The castle has two donjons ("Bergfried").

The castle is perhaps best known for its literary connection to William Shakespeare's tragedy Macbeth, the title character of which was made Thane of Cawdor. However, the story is highly fictionalised, and the castle itself was built many years after the events of the play.
Dit kasteel speelt een belangrijke rol in de film Shogun.

area: South Europe, Alps, Holy Roman Empire

castle_name: Castelo do Sabugal (Castelo das Cinco Quinas)

remarkable: Roofburcht, veelal in conflict met Zwolle en de bisschoppen van Utrecht.
description: Het kasteel is in de 16de eeuw in opdracht van prins Maurits ontmanteld. De ronde donjon is het enige orginele overblijfsel van het oorspronkelijke kasteel.
Gebouwd op de plaats waar in de 11de eeuw een motteburcht stond. Het kasteel met voorburcht is in de 19de-eeuw gerestaureerd en uitgebreid met de Ijzertoren en een kruisgang.

remarkable: De graven van Gelre woonden hier in de 14de- en 15de-eeuw.
description: De ronde donjon is de grootste in Nederland, 16 meter in doorsnede. Het vierkante huis ernaast en de bijgebouwen stammen uit de 18de-eeuw.

castle_name: Castillo de Cox (Castillo de Ayala, Castillo de Santa Bárbara)

description: De woonvleugel tussen de donjon en de poorttoren is in de 17de-eeuw toegevoegd. In het begin van de 20ste-eeuw is de donjon met een verdieping verhoogd.
Dit was het eerste Japanse kasteel met een hoge donjon op een stenen basis. Het is 6 jaar na de bouw in vlammen opgegaan. De donjon was waarschijnlijk 7 verdiepingen hoog.
In de 14de eeuw werd het kasteel verwoest. In de 15de eeuw werd het opnieuw opgebouwd. In de 18de eeuw werden de achterste weermuren en torens gesloopt.
First stone castle in Latvia.

castle_name: Achaia (Kefalonia, Venetian castle of Agios Georgios)
Palacio de los Duques de Feria (Zafra)

description: The castle was first built in 1068 to help William the Conqueror maintain control over the city. It is perched on an ancient volcanic plug, overlaying remains of the Roman city of Isca Dumnoniorum. The castle is named after the red stone found in the hill, and used in the construction of the original buildings, of which only ruins of the gatehouse remain.

remarkable: Bombed in WW I.
description: Converted to renaissance and romantic style.

area: Eastern Europe, Holy Roman Empire, Crusades

remarkable: Only the façade and a tower has been build. After the death of the baron the castle has never been finished.
description: De façade with its impressive entrance is huge: 200 metres long and 38 metres heigh.
Castle of Silesian dukes, then king of Czech, destroyed during 17th century warswith Sweden and Austria, finally abandoned in 1728

remarkable: The ruins of Old Rabenstein above the castle date from the 12th century.

The castle is mostly known for the ghastly Nyköping Banquet which took place here in 1317. The Nyköping banquet was king Birger of Sweden's Christmas celebration December 11, 1317 at the Nyköping Castle. Among the guests were his two brothers Duke Valdemar and Duke Eric, who later that night were imprisoned and starved to death in the dungeon of Nyköping Castle.
The castle has become famous as the last residence of Archduke Franz Ferdinand of Austria , heir of the Austro-Hungarian throne, whose assassination in Sarajevo triggered World War I

description: Currently a hotel.

area: South Europe, Alps, Holy Roman Empire

description: The original 13th-century castle was developed between the 15th and 17th centuries and given Romantic additions in the 19th century. Recent restoration work helps to conjure up the atmosphere of the border castles
Probably the first castle build without a separate donjon.

area: South Europe, Alps, Holy Roman Empire

remarkable: The main tower has a triangular front to protect the tower against sapping and projectiles. This form is unique in the Netherlands and can also be found at some advanced castles like  Chateau Gaillard in France.

founder: Rhys ap Gruffydd, ruler of Deheubarth from 1155 to 1197
